title: Miracle in the Sky: Vesna's Incredible Survival
story: Vesna Vulovic, a Serbian air stewardess, experienced an unbelievable event on January 26, 1972. Her plane broke apart high above Czechoslovakia, sending her plummeting over 10,000 meters without a parachute. Amazingly, Vesna survived the fall, setting a world record. Though badly injured, her miraculous survival astounded doctors and made headlines worldwide. Remember, always follow safety rules when flying!

title: A Dazzling Discovery in South Africa

Its discovery marked a shining moment in history, proving that Earth still held incredible treasures waiting to be revealed.

title: Snowstorm Brings Chicago to a Standstill
story: Snowflakes swirled furiously as Chicago faced its biggest winter challenge yet. In 1967, a massive blizzard hit the Windy City on January 26, blanketing streets with an astounding 23 inches of snow. The storm's intensity caught everyone off guard. Despite the chaos, Chicagoans banded together, helping neighbors and showing true community spirit.

title: Captain Cook's Tasmanian Pit Stop
story: Ahoy, matey! Captain James Cook sailed into history on January 26, 1777. His ship, Resolution, dropped anchor in Van Diemen's Land (now called Tasmania) for a quick supply run. This wasn't just any voyage - it was Cook's third and final journey exploring the vast Pacific Ocean. Little did he know, this pit stop would become a footnote in his legendary adventures!
activity_question: What was the name of Captain Cook's ship?
bonus_fact: Tasmania was renamed in 1856 after the Dutch explorer Abel Tasman.

title: Yeehaw! The Dukes Race onto TV
story: Engines revved and car chases began as 'The Dukes of Hazzard' zoomed onto television screens in 1979. This action-packed show about the wild adventures of cousins Bo and Luke Duke in rural Georgia became a hit. Airing for the first time on January 26, it featured their iconic orange car, the General Lee, leaping over obstacles and outrunning the bumbling sheriff.
activity_question: What was the name of the iconic orange car in the show?
bonus_fact: The show ran for seven seasons, ending in 1985.

title: Billie Eilish's Grammy Sweep
story: Billie Eilish rocked the music world on January 26, 2020. The young singer-songwriter dominated the 62nd Grammy Awards, snagging an impressive five golden gramophones. Her haul included the top prizes: album, record, and song of the year. At just 18, Eilish's unique sound and style had clearly struck a chord with both fans and critics alike.
activity_question: What were the top prizes Billie Eilish won at the Grammy Awards?
activity_choices: Album, record, and song of the year
Best new artist and best pop vocal album
Record of the year and best pop solo performance
Song of the year and best music video
activity_answer: Album, record, and song of the year
follow_up_question: How old was Billie Eilish when she won these Grammy Awards?
